reviews.review_description:
An absolute example of what a VR game should be. Huge, varied, physics-driven, and packed with different gameplay styles. It feels like playing a whole collection of games connected by a single story. No matter how long you play, it never gets boring.

A true masterpiece and an easy recommendation for everyone. And if you love Bonelab, this is an absolute must-buy.

reviews.review_title: Paid full price and cant play
reviews.review_description: I just picked this up for 30 bucks and its broken for me.  I constantly load into the tutorial, even though my first time playing I made it to the first race safe.  But now the game loads into the tutorial and I cant get past the part we're you are supposed to spawn something after getting money. The gun doesn't have any options to spawn anything any more and I cant progress.  Waste of 30 bucks!
Not great for the price

For starters I love the gameplay of this game, except when it tries to move me when looking through a drawer, or box. This is the kind of movement issues that make people sick. It should not slide around, when I’m looking through stuff. 
       My second thing is that trash reload system you got. If you are going to make people reload as much as this game needs too. It needs to be on the body, not hit a button have it pop up. That’s a truly pathetic way of reloading. Needs to be revamped. As there is no clip on my left side. Which is where it should be. Truly one of my biggest issues with this game.
This game is good like the campaign. Physics are decent and I love the collectible system for props and stuff. There's just a few things like the final boss battles hard, and combat being meh.

reviews.review_title: One of the most memorable 2025 VR titles
reviews.review_description:
Probably the most zany sandboxy game that Ive played in VR.
Dont missunderstand there are still "levels" or scenees in the game that you can complete and replay.

Full of great ideas that keep you guessing whats gonna happen next.

Great humour and some super cool ideas and tonnes of little easter eggs dotted all over the place.
 
Its bound to be a cult classic (hopefully an out right success if people get on board with the game!)

developer_response.body: Really appreciate you taking the time to leave us such a great review! I'll pass it onto the rest of the team for sure!

reviews.review_description: I'm enjoying the hell out of this game so far but I really wish you could use both hands on a pistol. The amount of credits I'm finding all over the place is pretty cool I just can't wait to be able to spend them on a little bit more than what the spawn gun allows right now. Honestly other than the double-handling pistol issue I have I don't have any issues. Can't wait to see what comes next it seems like it's not the smallest game which is awesome. Oh yeah if you can make it somehow to where you can just grab ammo off of your body I could be wrong and there might be one I just haven't figured it out yet

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thanks for taking the time to leave a review, for the ammo, if you press the menu button on your non-dominant controller (the left), it'll open up your inventory that stores your ammo. Hopefully, that helps you out!

Update: you can use both hands on a pistol since the new patch! And btw about the spawn gun - you can use Catalogue function of the gun to collect things it can spawn (weapons, explosives etc.)

reviews.review_description:
Had a lot of fun/scares playing this one. I think the physics and interactables are amazing however I did find some of the scenes a little tedious. Regardless, you can tell the studio put a lot of heart and soul into this game so definitely support them if you can.

My gameply vid is here for anyone interested: https://youtu.be/n0WWYxs3HGU?si=eKP3VF_rLd_sYR5g

Perhaps it's just because I've played so many "realistic" games but everything about this game just feels crude and sloppy.  Movement, grabbing, jumping, ducking, shooting, and especially melee weapons - they are useless.  If this game were $10 then I could understand but something this expensive should be a whole lot more finished.  This game needs to go back in the oven for long time because it's not fully cooked by a long shot.

developer_response.body: Hey! Thanks for letting us know about your experience with Deep Cuts, we have some updates coming that might help improve your overall experience but if you could elaborate on how the movements, melee weapons, etc felt sloppy we'd love to know.

reviews.review_description: This game was super fun. I really enjoyed it, but there are moments in the gameplay which really frustrated me. This game really does suffer from not telling you what you need to do. Specifically in these two levels, which you fight the fish dude and then the last fight with Bowie. I wish it told you that you had to hit the area, it was annoying to do multiple times until I found out. They also don’t tell you how to use your spawn gun, (if they did then I missed it and that’s on me) but I didn’t know how to save items into my spawn gun.. Ifound out it was ‘catalogue mode’ and even then, sometimes I just can’t scan it. I’m still really not sure how to use it. One last thing, are there more extra levels? I only have 3 (sandbox map, towers, and skate) I played two of them and they were just white boxes and ramps which was really disappointing to me. Other than these issues, I had a really good time with this game and I’d love to see more from these developers

This game actually quenched my Bonelab thirst after quite the absence of games like this. I will say that it's on par with bonelab, minus the extreme attention to detail on hand/weapon interaction, it's close but lackluster in some ways. However it had alot more humor and stuff going on. I find the variety fun enough to recommend.

But where's the ammo pouch? Ammo is stored in the inventory.. too cumbersome in my opinion, too many presses to get to the mags. Would love to see a swap locomotion stick option. I as a lefty is forced to use the right handed mode because lefty mode swaps the locomtion sticks.. or better yet introduced an ammo pouch to be on either left or right players choice. 

Get the ammo pouch and fix the ability to two-hand also the starter pistol.

Then you'll have my 5th star. Cheers!

Deep Cuts is a wild mix of madness, cinema, and incredible gameplay. You literally step into movies, living through scenes—from '80s slashers to retro sci-fi. The combat is dynamic, the atmosphere is top-notch, and the story pulls you in. This isn’t just a game; it’s a full-on cinematic adventure where you’re the star. If you love unique concepts and pure adrenaline, Deep Cuts is a must-play!

Deep Cuts offers a captivating blend of a physics-driven adventure with elements reminiscent of Warioware. While the game does present some challenges and glitches typical of a fully realised physics environment in VR, this is a common trait within the genre and not unique to Deep Cuts. However, when everything aligns perfectly, it creates an exhilarating experience that few other genres can match, delivering a thrilling sense of joy. Scythe Dev Team has cleverly woven a narrative that brings various film genres to life, crafting Warioware-inspired mini-games tailored to each movie. This approach not only keeps the gameplay engaging but also ensures that the environments you explore remain dynamic. If you have a penchant for physics-based games, you’ll appreciate the sandbox style that allows you to introduce unlocked weapons from different films into any movie, offering fresh and inventive ways to tackle the mini-games. Coupled with a robust Sandbox mode, there are countless reasons to dive into Deep Cuts for more excitement.

reviews.review_title: One of the best titles of 2025
reviews.review_description: This game has plenty of action! This is a sandbox type of game. You can use whatever to your disposal to complete the level. The whole time while I played this game I felt this was a bonelabs type of game. It was that good. BE SURE TO SCAN THE WEAPONS AND AMMO! This way you can spawn the ammo and guns whenever you want. Just keep collecting those credits! I highly recommend this game to all of you. Give it a try.

Closest thing to a bone works game as we’ve gotten since bone lab. a VR masterpiece in my opinion. these bad reviews make zero sense this game is awesome.  Silent drop zero advertising I was surprised I didn’t know this game existed or was in production.?!??? Loving it thank you devs I’ve been bored lately until now.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for this heartwarming review! We are glad the game has appeared on your radar anyway ^^
Tell your friends! ;)

reviews.review_title: The best intro I could’ve hoped for!
reviews.review_description:
Deep Cuts has been my introduction to the VR space and what a perfect introduction allowing me to fully immerse myself into this Korine-esc insane world, putting the headset on transports you into numerous different worlds all more acid painted than the next. 

I don’t know when I’ll ever get to finish this as there’s way too much to explore, I’m looking to every small detail, every hidden joke or reference and slowly turning round every corner. This is an experience to savour.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LBsRPvf1wEk

I wishlisted this game a while back, but never saw much about it for some reason. Just launched it and played for about an hour and a half, and wow. This game is amazing. It's super polished, and comparable to bonelab. Where it may fall behind in a few areas, like weapon selection (which is still being updated), it actually improves upon others, having a much more full story, great sense of humor, and impressive world (I spent over 20 minutes watching fake adds on a tv in one of the starting areas). Can't wait to see what's next, and I can't wait for the new updates

Deep Cuts is a game that throws players into a chaotic sandbox environment that’s interwoven to the brim with so much personality and character that is exemplified by the intriguing story, voice acting that puts you in the middle of the shenanigans that features a plethora of movie and pop culture references and all sorts of Easter eggs that players will be keen to discover.

The game wears it’s influences proudly on it’s sleeves as Deep Cuts is a cosmic gumbo of the open hub world exploration “collectathons” that we grew up on the Nintendo 64 era of gaming with the frantic and fast paced and responsive gunplay that we’ve grown accustomed to in modern gaming.

I wish there was more tutorial. I know how to save ammo (put it over your shoulder) but i can’t figure out how to retrieve it. I also can’t figure out how to use catalog mode on the gun. If someone could help me out i would be extremely grateful 
and would change to 5 stars. Message me if you have any answers.
Edit:The devs reached out super fast and cleared up all confusion. 10/10 game. GET IT NOW

developer_response.body: Hey there! To retrieve ammo for the gun you're currently holding in your hand, you open the inventory belt (menu button on the left controller), and the ammo clip will be there. Catalog mode on the bodegun is activated by pressing the Catalogue button on the gun's screen or pressing B/Y. Hope this helps and thank you for liking the game!

reviews.review_title: Loved it! only a few fixable problems
reviews.review_description: great game, loved the setting and story, just a few small issues. the entire space movie setting utterly pisses me off. its not a bad story, one of the better ones, just the way its set up makes it a pain to play. please make instructions more clear, for example i got stuck on the maze level cause i couldnt read the code, and i didnt realize i had to use the switch on the second space level cause the instructions didnt show. A bit more direction would be appreciated. Also, i think you should add achivements, not nessecery just a nice touch that could be added seamlessly with all the colectables

reviews.review_title: Not done yet but don’t regret buying
reviews.review_description: This game is awesome the graphics are cool I especially liked the old fashioned one the clown level scared the hell out of me it gave me a huge chill but it was still great it is very expensive sadly but it is still one heck of a game keep it up devs

reviews.review_title: Worlds best VR mission physics game!!!
reviews.review_description:
Keep up with the good work dev! 
The thing is that I am a physics gamer and learned 1000s of physical education in video games like Bonelab, the thing I don’t like about the game is the NPCs look like a robot zombie is there a reason why they look like that not trying to be rude I like the NPC models it still looks cool in all, but, it just looks a little creepy to be honest. So yeah I love this game.

reviews.review_title: I love this game but I'm having problems
reviews.review_description: This game is very fun if you liked the boneworks series. I am really enjoying the story and I am really enjoying the level designs. My one critique is that I cannot spawn things for some reason and I don't know if it is a bug or if I'm doing something wrong, but I literally cannot spawn any ammo for my guns.( If you guys know anything about this and if anybody has ever had this happen to them. Please tell me )

developer_response.body: Hey! Thanks for the kind words, we're glad you're enjoying the game, but that's definitely a strange bug. First make sure you're using 'Catalogue' mode to catalogue ammo or any items you find, then you can select the category and fire to spawn anything as you please! (You'll need some credits, though)

reviews.review_description:
I'm a huge bonelab player and this game is similar.

Pros :
Better story 
Physics feel smoother 


Cons: 
Graphics are not as detailed as bonelab. Feels like a graphic engine from 2001 

Can't customize your character. Character is boring 

What's the deal with not allowing me to holster items? 

Reloading guns sux. You have to bring up a menu to grab from a belt pack. Should let you pull a clip from your hip instead.

Physics based game but nothing in it reacts based on physics. The game is so boring at the beginning repetitive opening drawers to pick up cash. Still haven't started the first scene. I don't know how this has got the rating it has cause so far it's pretty terrible unless something happens real soon that changes my mind I'll be getting a refund. I was looking forward to this game. What a let down. This sucks

This game has a lot of personality and flair that set it apart from Bonelab but the moment i tried swinging an axe i knew I couldn't play this. Like i get that we're playing as a woman but c'mon. I should at least be able to do that. Plus they don't give you the ability to grab ledges so you're stuck trying to pull yourself up just to fall. If the physics keep me from enjoying the game then you've gone way too far in the realism department. The way you hold weapons also seems needlessly convoluted. Like just let me hold my weapon on my back or my hip instead of teleporting everything inside my body.
Very impressive, graphics are really good for standalone, gives you bonelabs/bonework vibes, fun game,.... Lights, camera, action

developer_response.body: Hey! Thank you so much for your feedback and the 5 stars! Really appreciate you taking the time

reviews.review_description: Talk about a unique concept for a game! Such a vibe, boneworks type interactions and great writing
I put a review out on like the day after it was released and the issues I listed were fixed in a couple days. Nice job dev team
This game is awesome. The story everything about this. Game is sweet I mean sweet love you guys thanks for the best vr game ever 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟
Game feels very poorly made and just cheap in general. Not worth the $30 there charging. Worth 15-20 bucks at most. Only get this if it’s on sale. Worst 30 bucks spent on a game.

developer_response.body: Sorry you had a bad experience with our game, do you mind elaborating on what you felt seemed cheap/poorly made?

reviews.review_title: Love happy humble burger farm and this
reviews.review_description: I loved the game in the title ￼so when i found out the same people made this i had to get it 5 stars!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for leaving us the 5 stars and playing ours games, the team really appreciate you!

reviews.review_description: The physics are similar to boneworks and the theme and just overall game is amazing please play this game
The game is already fire but so many better things can happen if it was multiplayer
It’s fun, worth it in my opinion

Game keeps me stuck in tutorial. I try to use cash to spawn but nothing happens. Just an error sound. This all happened after I saved the game using the shredder. I used the shredder and it took me back to main menu tutorial. What am I missing here?

Edit: 5 stars now. Reinstalled game and it works as intended.

developer_response.body:
Hey! You might be missing credits that you have to pick up before spawning a prop. There are some credits on that tutorial step on the table to the right. Also please choose Props on the spawner gun screen just to be sure. Please let us know if that helped!
The shredder in fact SHREDDS your save file, not saves it. The game has auto-save. This shredding is for those who want to start the game from the beginning ^^
